message:
chore(beads): close ft-3wow6 as-resolved

Tick-30 coordination concern cleared naturally: pane 2's ft-eu0no
(f540a5d5), pane 3's ft-yj375 (4d26269f), and pane 4's TOON work
(62744f55 + 0f744567) all landed without the predicted rebase
explosion. PolicyEngine::authorize receiver stayed &mut self so the
main API-drift risk didn't materialize.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3l2p1","title":"[security][P3] web server has no auth middleware; allow_public_bind exposes /events + /search + /panes + /stream/* without credentials","description":"## Finding

crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs build_app wires three middlewares — BodySizeGuard, RequestSpanLogger, StateInjector — and NO authentication middleware. The router exposes /health, /panes, /events, /search, /bookmarks, /ruleset-profile, /saved-searches, /stream/events, /stream/deltas as unauthenticated GETs.

web/server.rs:18 + :67 gate binding on non-localhost behind Config.allow_public_bind, and log a 'remotely reachable' warning. But once the operator flips that opt-in (to expose the SSE feeds to a dashboard on another host, for example), every captured pane byte — which may include agent prompts containing API keys, session tokens, PII, or secrets not yet caught by the Redactor — is served to any network client.

/search and /events can exfiltrate entire captured output history via FTS5 queries; /stream/deltas is a live firehose.

## Recommended fix

Either:
(a) Forbid allow_public_bind entirely until bearer-token auth middleware lands.
(b) Add a ShareToken middleware gated on Config.require_auth (default=true when allow_public_bind=true), checking an HMAC'd bearer token against a rotating secret.
(c) Require TLS + client-cert auth when bind is non-localhost.

(a) is smallest diff and most defensive for an early-stage project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- allow_public_bind=true without an auth config fails at startup, not at request time.
- Tests demonstrate localhost binds still work with no auth (no regression for the default case).

## Severity rationale
P3 because allow_public_bind is already an explicit opt-in and the 'remotely reachable' warning logs at startup. Still a real-world footgun: an operator chasing 'why won't my dashboard work?' is likely to flip the bind without realising there is zero auth. Not P0/P1 because default config is safe.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /security-audit-for-saas sweep, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:31.655369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:27.884427Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:27.883710Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 3b31a3d1: web server now fails closed on non-localhost bind because the current web API has no auth boundary","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","security","web"]}
 {"id":"ft-3l5bu","title":"[MEDIUM] RateLimiter.pane_counts HashMap leaks entries for every (pane_id, action) pair that is ever checked","description":"# Defect

## File + line

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:2103` — `RateLimiter::check`.

## Concrete failure path

Every `check(action, Some(pane_id))` call executes

    let timestamps = self.pane_counts.entry((pane_id, action)).or_default();

which **always** inserts a `Vec<Instant>` under `(pane_id, action)` if it doesn't exist, before any limit logic runs. Same story for `global_counts` keyed by `ActionKind`.

After a pane is destroyed, nothing removes its entries. `prune_old(timestamps, window_start)` removes expired timestamps from the Vec but leaves the Vec itself and the HashMap entry in place. Over the lifetime of a long-running `PolicyEngine`, the HashMap grows monotonically:

* Bound per pane: at most one entry per `ActionKind` variant (finite enum).
* Bound across panes: `unique(pane_id) × |ActionKind|`.

On a 200+-pane swarm churning through ~1000 panes/day over 90 days, that's ~90k × |ActionKind| entries at ~16 bytes key + baseline Vec — not a crash, but a monotonic leak tracked nowhere. **Same class as ft-l6v1r** (`pane_activity_tracker`) and the `BackpressureMetrics::dropped_by_pane` leak I fixed earlier (f2390da9).

## Worse-case path: pane_id reuse

The ft-l6v1r commit observed that wezterm can reuse pane_ids. If a reused pane_id hashes to an old (pane_id, action) entry whose Vec still contains stale timestamps, the new pane inherits the dead pane's rate-limit budget — either under-counted (if the prune pass has already emptied the Vec) or over-counted (if timestamps haven't aged out yet). The current `check` path prunes based on `window_start` which is relative to `Instant::now()`, so stale timestamps from a destroyed pane will age out correctly — but entries without any timestamps will still mis-attribute the **first** rate-limited hit back to the old pane's history in any diagnostic that reads `pane_counts` directly.

## Fix scope

1. `RateLimiter::cleanup_pane(pane_id) -> usize` — remove every `(pane_id, _)` entry from `pane_counts`, return the number removed for telemetry / debug_assert. Cheap: one full iteration.
2. Unit test pinning: entries present before, zero after, unrelated panes untouched, idempotent on panes never inserted.
3. Wiring into the PaneDestroyed teardown path is a separate follow-up bead — `PolicyEngine` is not currently plumbed through `ObservationRuntime::spawn_discovery_task` or `handle_native_event`, and that plumbing is a broader cross-boundary change that belongs on its own bead.

Single-file scope: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:26.564595Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:31.244870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:54:31.244127Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"ft-3wow6","title":"[review][coord][LOW] 3 panes editing overlapping surfaces — pane 2 mcp_tools.rs + pane 3 main.rs + pane 4 test-goldens — first-to-land forces rebase","description":"Coordination snapshot at reviewer round 22, 2026-04-23. Three panes currently hold uncommitted edits to files that share the same working tree:

## Pane 2 ft-eu0no (shared PolicyEngine / rate limiter fix for my HIGH ft-eu0no)

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (+77 / -20 hunks) — likely extracting RateLimiter into Arc/Mutex or adding shared-state accessors
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs (+20 / -3) — likely constructing shared engine at server build
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs (+60 / -14, 10 hunks) — rewiring every per-call build_policy_engine site
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs — potentially for audit-record plumbing related to ft-6mmyp follow-up ft-h90rh

## Pane 3 ft-yj375 (wa.state + robot state auth)

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs — CLI entry for ft robot state
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs — regression test

## Pane 4 TOON conformance + ft-2zbie regen

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs — canonicalize + harness updates
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_mission_toon_mcp_conformance.rs (NEW) — TOON parallel harness
- 9 golden_robot_envelope/wa_mission_*.json + wa_tx_*.json — regenerated from placeholders

## Conflict risk inventory

- **mcp_tools.rs** — ONLY pane 2 touching it. No direct conflict with pane 3 or 4.
- **main.rs** — ONLY pane 3 touching it. No direct conflict with pane 2.
- **policy.rs** — ONLY pane 2. Isolated.
- **storage.rs** — ONLY pane 2 (probably).
- **.beads/issues.jsonl** — ALL three panes + this reviewer touch it. JSONL is line-appendable; merges usually clean but can produce duplicate entries or re-ordered lines.

## Real risk: API signature drift

If pane 2's ft-eu0no changes PolicyEngine::authorize's receiver from &mut self to &self (plausible if the rate limiter moves into Arc<Mutex>), pane 3's in-flight main.rs edits may call authorize with the OLD signature. Second-to-land rebase hits a compile break. Compile breaks are recoverable; the risk is the 'verification partial' pattern we've seen 5+ times this session — the second pane may commit without re-running tests post-rebase.

## Concrete coordination step for first-to-land

- Whoever lands first: in the commit message, state the new PolicyEngine::authorize signature explicitly (if changed) so the second pane can rebase mechanically.
- Whoever lands second: re-run the file-specific cargo check before committing. Not the full test suite — just cargo check --lib -p frankenterm-core. Catches any signature drift within ~30s.
- Whoever lands third: same rule.

## Why LOW, not MEDIUM

- No silent-correctness risk; conflicts are compile-time visible.
- The three panes' edits don't mathematically overlap on source lines.
- Filing primarily so a post-hoc audit ('why did three consecutive verification-partial commits land') has a pointer to the coordination window.

Tag: review, coordination.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:59.146818Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:10:02.467910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:10:02.467685Z","close_reason":"Resolved naturally — all three tick-30 in-flight panes landed without the predicted rebase explosion:

- Pane 2 ft-eu0no landed in f540a5d5 (mcp.rs +22, mcp_bridge.rs +118/-, policy.rs +110 — the actual touch set was slightly different from the bead's prediction: mcp_tools.rs got pane 2's rate-limiter threading via ride-along in my subsequent ft-rsqap/ft-mw1zb/ft-zo3cr commits rather than a standalone pane 2 commit).
- Pane 3 ft-yj375 landed in 4d26269f (main.rs + wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs).
- Pane 4 TOON work landed in 62744f55 (mission) and 0f744567 (tx).

Bead's main risk concern (PolicyEngine::authorize receiver changing from &mut self to &self under the SharedRateLimiter refactor) did not materialize — pane 2 kept authorize(&mut self) and wrapped the rate limiter state in Arc<Mutex> without changing the method signature.

What kept it safe:
(1) Natural serialization — panes committed in order of readiness rather than racing, so each landing became HEAD before the next pane's commit went in.
(2) Explicit ride-along flagging in commit messages for files touched by multiple panes (see 810ad4c9, c3d273f0, 97016fe6 all flag pane 2's mcp_tools.rs hunks) preserved attribution when uncommitted work rode along.
(3) Per-pane narrow reservations via Agent Mail avoided compounding blast radius when reservations were flaky.

Remaining open concern (NOT this bead's scope): four recent commits land with 'verification partial' notes citing rch/cargo infra unreliability — f540a5d5, cec75f71, 03eb5831, 8d1e93dc. That's an infra quality issue, not a multi-pane coordination one, and should be its own bead if a separate pane hasn't filed it yet. Separate concern left for ops triage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-3zvmp","title":"retry jitter can exceed max_delay after pre-jitter cap","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:09.235064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.710904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.710537Z","close_reason":"Landed in 464ad9b4 (fix(retry): clamp jittered delays to max_delay)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-45805","title":"[mor:ergonomics] rch PreToolUse hook SIGTERMs cargo; failure mode should be 'fallback', not 'die'","description":"# Observation

From working memory + user experience across recent sessions:

- rch PreToolUse hook (\`force_remote=true\` in
  ~/.config/rch/config.toml) intercepts every Bash call containing
  \`cargo\` and routes to remote workers.
- When remote workers are unhealthy, the subprocess receives SIGTERM
  (exit 143).
- Local fallback exists only via workarounds (Python fork+setsid,
  \`enabled=false\` override, \`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<name>\`
  bypass).

At 3am: operator needs to reproduce a test failure. Runs
\`cargo test foo\`. Shell returns exit 143 with no diagnostic.
Operator has to know the workaround exists. New operators or less-
ranked agents don't know the workaround and waste 30 minutes
re-trying.

This isn't frankenterm code, but it is "the development
environment frankenterm runs inside," and it breaks
frankenterm-the-project's ergonomics during incident response.

# Question

Two levers worth exploring:

1. **Fallback-on-worker-failure in rch itself.** If the remote
   workers SIGTERM the subprocess, catch the signal, run locally,
   mark the worker unhealthy. Exit 143 becomes a last resort,
   not the default failure mode.

2. **Project-level \`just\` or \`make\` target** that wraps the
   known-good build recipe: \`CC=...clang CXX=...clang++
   CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-local-target cargo <args>\`. The 3am
   operator runs \`just test foo\` and it always works — no memory
   of which env vars to set.

Concrete ask: would a one-line \`just build\` / \`just test\`
target in the frankenterm root, with the fork-bypass recipe
already baked in, materially improve the 3am experience? It's
~15 min of work and saves 30 min per incident per operator.

# 3am principle

Every workaround that lives only in memory (mine, yours, the
next-agent's) is a booby-trap for the on-call. Encode the
workaround in a target; when it stops being necessary, the target
becomes a no-op one-liner.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:36:41.686932Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.070631Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:54.611695Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["3am","ergonomics","mor","rch","tooling"]}
@@ -137,7 +137,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-b9ndv","title":"[fuzz] add MCP request params parser harness","description":"Existing fuzz coverage already hits pattern-pack TOML/binary, config TOML, workflow YAML, robot envelope JSON, IPC auth envelope, snapshot deserialization, replay recordings, and term/escape parsers. But there is still no fuzz target for MCP request-param parsing.

Evidence:
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no MCP parser target; current targets stop at `ipc_auth_envelope`, `robot_envelope_json`, and related config parsers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:25-170` defines the externally-controlled MCP request param structs (`StateParams`, `GetTextParams`, `SearchParams`, `EventsParams`, etc.), but they are `pub(super)` and only covered by unit/proptest deserialization checks.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:1170-1845` has property tests over many `serde_json::from_value(...)` paths, but no byte-level fuzzing against malformed/truncated/oversized JSON.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_framework.rs:203-265` also round-trips untrusted remote MCP payloads through `serde_json::from_value(...)`.

Why it matters:
- MCP is a network/external-input surface with many optional/defaulted fields and multiple tool-specific param shapes.
- Proptests cover valid structured values, not adversarial raw JSON bytes, duplicate keys, deep nesting, type confusion, or truncation.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz seam plus harness that feeds raw JSON/Value payloads through the MCP request-param decode paths (or framework payload bridge) and asserts no panic / deterministic decode outcomes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.039115Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:48.038906Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4561eac1: add MCP request params fuzz harness and seed corpus","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-bekwl","title":"[tx][compensation] PaneStepExecutor compensation path returns synthetic success without executing rollback actions","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs:633-673`, `PaneStepExecutor::execute_compensations()` has no access to the contract compensation plan, leaves `contract_compensations` empty, and returns `success: true` / `reason_code: "compensation_succeeded"` for each committed step unless a deterministic injected failure was requested. The comments explicitly admit this is synthetic reporting rather than real rollback.

Impact: tx rollback can report success even when no compensation action actually ran.

Fix direction: thread contract compensation actions into the executor/execution engine so compensation dispatch can execute real `StepAction`s instead of emitting synthetic success records.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:09:03.406659Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:41.670498Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:41.670191Z","close_reason":"Fully fixed by 74a775bc (ft-npj7t). Verified inspection of tx_execution.rs:629-685: (a) execute_compensations now takes &MissionTxContract, (b) contract_compensations HashMap populated from contract.plan.compensations, (c) real execute_step_action call replaces synthetic success on line 679-682, (d) missing compensation returns fail-closed FTX_COMPENSATION_MISSING, (e) integration test at :3567-3589 asserts mock pane content contains 'ROLLBACK' after rollback (proving end-to-end dispatch). All four elements of the original ft-bekwl claim are now FALSE. Duplicate of ft-npj7t.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-biafh","title":"[audit-note] runtime.rs deadlock-finder no finding","description":"Deadlock-finder-and-fixer pass on crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs completed on 2026-04-23. Reviewed relay/persistence handoff, native-event coalescer, lock ordering, and shutdown/task-join paths. No CRITICAL/HIGH deadlock, starvation, lost-wakeup, or unbounded-buffer defect with a concrete repro was found in runtime.rs during this pass.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:18:02.424603Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:49.325248Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:49.324937Z","close_reason":"Audit note recorded; no finding from deadlock-finder pass on runtime.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-bkgbq","title":"[architecture][follow-up ft-zo3cr] Centralize PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in main.rs","description":"## Context

ft-zo3cr identified an architectural smell: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` repeats the PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in at least 4 branches with slightly-different defaults:

- :6992-6999 — PolicyEngine::new + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (bare, for a single policy check)
- :15341-15359 — PolicyEngine::permissive + PolicyGatedInjector + CxPolicyInjector + WorkflowEngine + PaneWorkflowLockManager (full workflow runtime stack)
- :19850-19869 — PolicyEngine::new(require_prompt_active) + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (similar to :6992 but with injector+storage)
- :25526-25540 — PolicyEngine::new(true) + with_tuning BUT OMITS with_command_gate_config and with_policy_rules (drift!)

ft-zo3cr in-scope fix (landed in ddf4eb8f) closed a similar drift in mcp_tools.rs:2558. The main.rs sites were left out because this architectural bead scoped only crates/frankenterm-core/* via its reservation.

## Why this matters

The :19850 vs :25526 comparison is the exact kind of drift the bead warns about: one call site gates on command_gate + policy_rules, the other doesn't. Any future fix to one doesn't land in the other. The :19846-19849 comment in current code already records a past regression of this shape (workflow injector diverged from the direct send path).

## Suggested direction (matches the bead's original proposal)

Introduce a single composition factory in frankenterm-core (or a dedicated module under crates/frankenterm/src/) exposing shape-typed builders:

    fn build_human_send_stack(config: &Config) -> HumanSendStack { ... }
    fn build_mcp_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: StorageHandle, wezterm: ...) -> McpWorkflowStack { ... }
    fn build_robot_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: ...) -> RobotWorkflowStack { ... }

Each returns a bundle (PolicyEngine + Injector + optional Runner). Handlers at the 4 main.rs sites become declarative — they ask for the bundle rather than open-code the assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 main.rs sites uses one of the shared factories.
- The feature set (tuning / command_gate / policy_rules / require_prompt_active) applied by each factory is asserted by a focused unit test (one per bundle shape).
- Any future config knob added to PolicyEngine has exactly ONE place to thread through.

## Scope note

Requires crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs reservation (not in ft-zo3cr's scope). Best picked up after any outstanding main.rs work (e.g. other rate-limiter or safety-config refactors) lands.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-zo3cr's mcp_tools.rs drift fix, 2026-04-23.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:10.623144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:10.623144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","cli","follow-up","strategic"]}
+{"id":"ft-bkgbq","title":"[architecture][follow-up ft-zo3cr] Centralize PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in main.rs","description":"## Context

ft-zo3cr identified an architectural smell: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` repeats the PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in at least 4 branches with slightly-different defaults:

- :6992-6999 — PolicyEngine::new + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (bare, for a single policy check)
- :15341-15359 — PolicyEngine::permissive + PolicyGatedInjector + CxPolicyInjector + WorkflowEngine + PaneWorkflowLockManager (full workflow runtime stack)
- :19850-19869 — PolicyEngine::new(require_prompt_active) + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (similar to :6992 but with injector+storage)
- :25526-25540 — PolicyEngine::new(true) + with_tuning BUT OMITS with_command_gate_config and with_policy_rules (drift!)

ft-zo3cr in-scope fix (landed in ddf4eb8f) closed a similar drift in mcp_tools.rs:2558. The main.rs sites were left out because this architectural bead scoped only crates/frankenterm-core/* via its reservation.

## Why this matters

The :19850 vs :25526 comparison is the exact kind of drift the bead warns about: one call site gates on command_gate + policy_rules, the other doesn't. Any future fix to one doesn't land in the other. The :19846-19849 comment in current code already records a past regression of this shape (workflow injector diverged from the direct send path).

## Suggested direction (matches the bead's original proposal)

Introduce a single composition factory in frankenterm-core (or a dedicated module under crates/frankenterm/src/) exposing shape-typed builders:

    fn build_human_send_stack(config: &Config) -> HumanSendStack { ... }
    fn build_mcp_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: StorageHandle, wezterm: ...) -> McpWorkflowStack { ... }
    fn build_robot_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: ...) -> RobotWorkflowStack { ... }

Each returns a bundle (PolicyEngine + Injector + optional Runner). Handlers at the 4 main.rs sites become declarative — they ask for the bundle rather than open-code the assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 main.rs sites uses one of the shared factories.
- The feature set (tuning / command_gate / policy_rules / require_prompt_active) applied by each factory is asserted by a focused unit test (one per bundle shape).
- Any future config knob added to PolicyEngine has exactly ONE place to thread through.

## Scope note

Requires crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs reservation (not in ft-zo3cr's scope). Best picked up after any outstanding main.rs work (e.g. other rate-limiter or safety-config refactors) lands.

 {"id":"ft-btg0h","title":"[fuzz] add frankenterm/term Terminal::advance_bytes target for full escape-to-state pipeline","description":"Surface: frankenterm/term/src/terminal.rs:164 — pub fn advance_bytes<B: AsRef<[u8]>>(&mut self, bytes: B). Master terminal state-machine entry point: raw output bytes → frankenterm_escape_parser → Performer::perform (terminalstate/performer.rs:252) → every CSI/SGR/OSC/DCS dispatch path (perform_csi_mode/window/edit/cursor/sgr in terminalstate/mod.rs:1425-2648, plus image pipelines in terminalstate/sixel.rs, terminalstate/kitty.rs (37KB), terminalstate/iterm.rs). Existing escape_parser_raw fuzz target covers only the lexer stage (bytes → Action); termwiz_csi_parser covers the escape-parser CSI roundtrip property. Nothing exercises the state-machine mutations, cursor/edit bounds, or the Sixel/Kitty/iTerm2 image decoders under arbitrary input.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs — construct a Terminal with a minimal TerminalConfiguration impl, 24x80 size, cap input at 256 KB, feed the whole slice through advance_bytes, drop the terminal. No assertions — libFuzzer asserts no-panic / no-OOM / no-unbounded-alloc.

Corpus seeds: plain text, ESC[H clear, SGR sequences, DECSET/DECRST modes, DECRQM, OSC 52 clipboard, OSC 8 hyperlinks, bracketed paste, CSI u keyboard, DCS sixel fragment (\\x1bPq#0;2;100;0;0#0~~\\x1b\\\\), kitty APC image fragment, iTerm2 OSC 1337 file upload preamble, overlong / truncated / interleaved-with-text variants.

Target must survive 256 KB input without: panic, unbounded allocation (scrollback bounded at 64), unterminated DCS hang, arithmetic overflow in cursor arithmetic, OOB cell index. This discovers sixel/kitty image-decoder arithmetic bugs and any CSI dispatch path that does not clamp its operand to grid bounds.

Add to fuzz/Cargo.toml: frankenterm-term = { path = "../frankenterm/term" }. No new features required — Terminal::advance_bytes is in the crate public API for terminal hosts.

Do NOT duplicate: ft-t4n0z (termwiz InputParser, closed), ft-he0w7 (native_events, closed), ft-p2bjm (replay, in flight), escape_parser_raw (lexer stage only), termwiz_csi_parser (CSI roundtrip property on escape-parser crate, not term::Terminal).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:08:20.022220Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.992943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.992183Z","close_reason":"Landed in 168d379a (term_advance_bytes harness + corpus); fuzz-build linker blocker documented on bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","security"],"comments":[{"id":3618,"issue_id":"ft-btg0h","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented local diff for term_advance_bytes fuzz harness in fuzz/Cargo.toml, fuzz/fuzz_targets/term_advance_bytes.rs, and fuzz/corpus/term_advance_bytes/*.

Build blocker on verification lane:
1. rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-DarkAnchor-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo +nightly fuzz build term_advance_bytes
2. rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-DarkAnchor-target CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_AARCH64_APPLE_DARWIN_LINKER=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ cargo +nightly fuzz build term_advance_bytes

Both runs reach the final link for bin term_advance_bytes and fail with the same libfuzzer/c++ symbol error:
Undefined symbols for architecture arm64:
  std::__1::__hash_memory(void const*, unsigned long)
from liblibfuzzer_sys ... FuzzerLoop.o

The second run proves the explicit clang++ linker override does not fix it. Keeping bead open pending toolchain/libfuzzer resolution or alternate verified build lane.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:20:12Z"}]}

Verification: rch cargo test blocked on remote CC=/opt/homebrew/... path missing on Linux worker; local cargo test reached unrelated existing compile errors in vendored/mux_client.rs and web_framework.rs after this slice compiled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-eqtcq","title":"aegis_entropy_anomaly::EProcess::update propagates NaN e_value; permanently disables rejection","description":"EProcess::update calls likelihood_ratio(h, baseline_mean, baseline_std, collapse_mean, collapse_std). If any f64 input is NaN, log_h0 and log_h1 both become NaN, log_lr = NaN - NaN = NaN, log_lr.exp() = NaN, and clamp(1e-6, 1e6) passes NaN through because f64::clamp does not reject NaN. self.e_value *= NaN = NaN. The final clamp(1e-100, max_e) at update:226 also passes NaN through. Future is_rejected() returns (NaN >= threshold) = false, silently disabling anomaly detection for the lifetime of this EProcess. Matches ft-b4l62 / ft-yskcu / ft-c17iw / ft-n4fdx pattern — fail-closed on non-finite inputs at the update() boundary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:02.334443Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:05:25.492018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:05:25.491748Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-eu0no","title":"[review][HIGH] PolicyEngine rate limiter fresh-per-MCP-request makes rate_limit config non-functional across all MCP tools","description":"Proactive policy.rs audit round 20. policy.rs:2077-2088 defines RateLimiter with state HashMaps pane_counts and global_counts owned BY the PolicyEngine struct. mcp_helpers.rs:97-107 build_policy_engine returns a fresh PolicyEngine every call (no Arc, no shared Mutex). grep confirms build_policy_engine is called at mcp_tools.rs:1130/1514/2029/2272/2332/3187/3319 and at the NEW ft-x86z2 helper at :187 — every MCP tool invocation constructs a fresh engine with empty HashMaps. Effect: config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane and rate_limit_global are non-functional for ALL MCP flows. Every request starts with current=0 less than limit=N so authorize never denies on rate grounds. ft-x86z2's new gate inherits the bug. Malicious MCP client can flood any mutation endpoint without backoff. Validation: build_policy_engine has no Arc/Mutex/thread_local sharing. RateLimiter HashMaps at policy.rs:2085-2087 are plain owned maps. 9 distinct build_policy_engine call sites in mcp_tools.rs each at per-request handler entry. Contrast: connector_outbound_bridge.rs:833 has set_policy_engine/policy_engine accessors holding a long-lived engine — long-lived is a known pattern, just not used for MCP. Fix: thread Arc-Mutex-PolicyEngine into each ToolHandler at construction, OR extract RateLimiter into Arc-Mutex-RateLimiter inside PolicyEngine. Acceptance: call wa.send N+1 times where N equals rate_limit_per_pane; assert N+1-th returns MCP_ERR_POLICY with rate-limit reason. Related ft-ym8nq LOW (deny-path not tested) would silently skip the rate-limit branch anyway. Severity HIGH: advertised defense that does not work. Audit round 20, 2026-04-23.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:27:18.613535Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:29.482554Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"],"comments":[{"id":3623,"issue_id":"ft-eu0no","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped structural fix in f540a5d5. MCP now shares rate-limit state across the server instance via Arc<Mutex<RateLimiter>> threading through policy.rs, mcp.rs, and mcp_bridge.rs. Verification is partial: the 100-request regression test exists in mcp_tools.rs and the narrow rch/cargo-local lanes reached compile phase, but I did not get a clean pass/fail result before the ship-or-surface cutoff. Verified by inspection; regression execution still pending.","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:58:05Z"},{"id":3624,"issue_id":"ft-eu0no","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped structural fix in f540a5d5. Verified by inspection; regression execution still pending.","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-eze48","title":"[fuzz] add FtviIndex::from_bytes harness for vector index loader","description":"The FTVI vector-index binary loader is public and currently unfuzzed.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/mod.rs:73` publicly re-exports `FtviIndex`.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:218-267` parses attacker-controlled bytes in `FtviIndex::from_bytes`, including magic/version checks, dimension/count headers, and per-record half-float decoding loops.
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no target for `FtviIndex::from_bytes`.

Why it matters:
- This is a binary format loader over persisted search state, so malformed bytes can hit allocation pressure, unexpected EOF handling, half-float edge cases, and count/dimension loops.
- The surface is already public, synchronous, and easy to fuzz without extra seams.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz target that exercises both arbitrary raw bytes and structured valid FTVI buffers via `write_ftvi_vec` round-trips, asserting parse no-panics and round-trip sanity where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331896Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.556262Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.555662Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 6567397b: add FtviIndex::from_bytes fuzz harness","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}
